candidates.question: Take 57 and scale it down by half. Then find the square root of the scaled number. Finally, calculate the ratio of that square root result to 21.
A patient weighing 93 kg needs a medication dosed at 7.5 mg per kg. Compute this dose, then round it to one decimal place. Finally, divide that rounded dose by 14.
Calculate the total minutes in 1 hour and 28 minutes. Then convert those minutes since midnight into a clock time. Lastly, extract the initials from that resulting time string.
Reverse the phrase 'breeze signal drift'. Next, convert the reversed phrase to title case. Finally, check if this new title-cased text is longer than 13 characters.

Calculate the calories burned by a 74 kg person exercising at an MET of 3 for 0.5 hours. Then find the absolute difference between that total and 302 calories. Finally, see if this difference exceeds 384.
